w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Turning acquaintances into close friends through shared interests,
spontaneous experiences, and memories worth keeping.
Discovery · Motives (inc. Journey Motives) · Chat · Profile
Phone mandatory + email magic link / Google Sign-In. Self-managed JWT.
Supabase Realtime (CDC over WebSocket). Chat + presence + live Motive updates.
~$0/month. All free tiers. No credit card required.
Every decision with rationale, costs, and migration paths

Own your data, rent your compute. The database schema, business logic, and auth live in code you control. The servers running that code are rented from whoever is cheapest and best at the time. Every component below has a documented migration path that does not require rewriting core business logic.
React Native + Expo SDK (managed workflow). TypeScript throughout. Expo Router is skipped in favour of React Navigation v6 for complex navigation state control.
React Navigation v6 — bottom tabs navigator with native-stack navigators per tab. Handles deep links from push notifications to specific Motive RSVP cards.
Zustand for client/UI state. TanStack Query (React Query v5) for server state — caching, background refetch, optimistic updates for chat and Motive interactions.
Expo SecureStore for refresh tokens (device secure enclave). MMKV for all other local state — 10× faster than AsyncStorage.
Reanimated 3 + Gesture Handler. React Native Bottom Sheet by Gorhom for creation flow modals. All animation specs from v2 doc remain unchanged.
EAS Build (30 builds/month free). EAS Update for OTA JavaScript updates without app store submission. EAS Submit for App Store / Play Store automation.
Expo Notifications SDK → APNs (iOS) / FCM (Android). Free. Abstracted behind a notification service layer — migrating to direct APNs/FCM is a 2-day task.
Node.js 20 LTS + Hono framework. TypeScript. Hono is modern, fast, and framework-agnostic — identical API runs on Node.js, Bun, or Cloudflare Workers.
Drizzle ORM. TypeScript-native schema definition, zero-overhead SQL generation, no binary runtime dependency (unlike Prisma). Faster cold starts on Fly.io.
Self-managed JWT. ~250 lines of code. Phone number entry mandatory → email magic link or Google Sign-In verification. No auth vendor. Zero lock-in.
Inngest Phase 1 (50k function runs/month free, managed, dashboard included). BullMQ on Upstash Redis Phase 2+ (more control, lower cost at scale).
Sharp on API server: resize photos on upload, convert to WebP, strip all EXIF metadata (removes GPS coordinates from photos). Satori for memory card image generation (JSX → SVG → PNG).
Supabase Postgres (free: 500MB). Standard PostgreSQL — pg_dump exports to any Postgres host. pg_trgm extension for username/name search.

Supabase Realtime — CDC (Change Data Capture) over WebSocket. Broadcasts Postgres INSERT/UPDATE events to subscribed clients. Handles chat, Motive updates, journey live view, venue voting. Free: 2M messages/month, 200 concurrent connections.
Supabase Presence (built on Realtime, free). Ephemeral state: typing indicators, online status, "I'm here" mode (V2 proximity feature). Auto-removes disconnected users.
Supabase Storage Phase 1 (free: 1GB, CDN via Cloudflare included). Cloudflare R2 Phase 2+ (10GB free, zero egress fees — critical for photo-heavy apps).
Upstash Redis serverless (free: 10k commands/day). Rate limiting counters, JWT blacklist, short-lived caches (FOF suggestions, daily prompt distribution).
Resend (free: 3k emails/month). Magic link emails + transactional. React Email for HTML templates. Standard SMTP — swap to Postmark/SendGrid in one service file.
PostHog Cloud (free: 1M events/month). Open-source — self-host if limits exceeded. Funnels, retention, feature flags, session replay. GDPR-compliant.
Sentry (free: 5k errors/month). React Native SDK with PII scrubbing. GlitchTip (open-source Sentry) as self-hosted fallback.
Google Places API ($200 free credit/month). Abstracted behind venues.service.ts — swap to Foursquare (100k free calls/month) without touching business logic.
Fly.io (free: 3 shared VMs). Docker-based deployment — zero Fly-specific code. Migrating to Railway / Render / GCP Cloud Run is a docker push.
Turborepo. Structure: apps/mobile (Expo), packages/api (Hono), packages/shared (TypeScript types + Drizzle schema). One repo, one tsconfig, shared types.

Auth libraries (Supabase Auth, Clerk, Auth0) are designed for standard email-first or OAuth flows. The hybrid flow required here — phone number collected first, email verification second, phone stored encrypted with SHA-256 hash for contact matching — requires significant customisation of any auth library. At that point, you are maintaining a plugin on top of a library rather than owning the code. Self-managed JWT is ~250 lines of code you fully control.
User enters phone number (E.164 format, validated client-side with libphonenumber-js). Sent to POST /auth/start with the phone number in the request body.
Validates phone format. Stores phone in pending_auth table with a 15-minute expiry. Returns a session_id (UUID). Does not create a users row yet.
Shows two options simultaneously: "Continue with Google" (primary) and email magic link (secondary). Google path is faster — no waiting for email.

Accepts {google_id_token, session_id}. Verifies Google token against Google's public keys. Retrieves phone from pending_auth via session_id. Upserts users row. Returns JWT access token (15 min) + refresh token (90 days, stored in refresh_tokens table as SHA-256 hash).

Accepts {email, session_id}. Generates 32-byte cryptographically random token. Stores SHA-256(token) in magic_link_tokens. Sends email via Resend with deep link: icebreaker://auth/verify?token=XXX. Rate limited: 3 per email per hour.
Accepts {token}. Hashes token, looks up in magic_link_tokens, checks expiry and single-use. Retrieves phone from pending_auth. Upserts users row. Returns JWT pair.

Accepts refresh token. Validates hash against refresh_tokens. Checks used_at — if already used, token reuse detected: invalidate entire family_id, force re-auth. Issues new access + refresh tokens. Marks old refresh token used_at.
Extracts Bearer token from Authorization header. Verifies JWT signature with server secret. Attaches user_id to req.user. Returns 401 if invalid or expired.

phone_number: TEXT encrypted at rest using pgcrypto (AES-256). Never returned in any API response. phone_hash: SHA-256(E.164_number + SERVER_PEPPER). Indexed. Used for contact matching. The SERVER_PEPPER is an environment secret — never in code or database.

Supabase Realtime is PostgreSQL Change Data Capture (CDC) over WebSocket. When a row is inserted or updated in a subscribed table, Supabase broadcasts the change to all connected clients on that table's channel. This means the database is the single source of truth for both persistence and real-time delivery — no separate message broker, no dual-write problem, no synchronisation complexity.

Client subscribes to the Realtime channel for their active chat_id. When a message is INSERTed to the messages table via the API, Supabase broadcasts the new row to all subscribers. Client receives it in under 100ms on good connections.

When a client reconnects after being offline, it calls GET /chats/:id/messages?since=[last_read_at]. Postgres returns all messages since the last seen message. No separate message queue or delivery guarantee system needed — Postgres is the queue.
Postgres INSERT timestamp (created_at) is the authoritative order. No logical clocks needed. Client renders messages sorted by created_at ascending.

Supabase Presence (not Realtime CDC). Client sends typing: true to the chat's Presence channel. Other clients see the Presence map update immediately. On stop typing or disconnect, Presence automatically removes the state. No database writes.
RSVP status changes, venue selection, stop transitions on Journey Motives — all broadcast via Realtime on the motives table channel. Client subscribes to Motives they're attending.
stop_checkins INSERT events broadcast to all Motive attendees in real-time. Journey progress indicator updates without polling.

When a new Motive is INSERTed, a Supabase database function (PL/pgSQL trigger) runs the collision detection query. If a collision is found, it INSERTs to motive_collisions, which triggers a Realtime broadcast to both creators' notification channels.

Supabase Realtime free tier: 2M messages/month, 200 concurrent connections. Triggered around 1,000 DAU with active chat. Pro plan ($25/month, included in Supabase Pro) raises this to 5M messages and 500 concurrent.

Socket.io gives you a real-time channel and nothing else. Message persistence, ordering, offline sync, multi-device delivery, and history pagination must all be built manually on top of Socket.io — that is 3–4 weeks of infrastructure work. Supabase Realtime gives you all of this as a property of the database you are already using. The only thing Supabase Realtime cannot do that Socket.io can: binary message streaming. You are not sending binary data in chat. This is not a tradeoff.

Drizzle is a TypeScript-first ORM that generates raw SQL with zero runtime overhead. The schema is defined in TypeScript — no separate .prisma files, no separate schema language. Drizzle is chosen over Prisma specifically because: no binary dependency (Prisma ships a query engine binary that adds cold start time on Fly.io), TypeScript types are derived from the schema automatically, and complex queries (FOF graph traversal, collision detection, prompt match ranking) can be written as typed raw SQL without losing type safety.
Before upload: compress to 80% quality, resize to max 2048px on longest edge. Reduces upload size by 60–80%. Runs on device — no server bandwidth wasted on oversized originals.

On receipt: validate MIME type from magic bytes (not Content-Type header). Resize profile photos to 400×400px, memory card photos to 1200px max width. Convert all images to WebP (30–50% smaller than JPEG). Strip ALL EXIF metadata — this removes GPS coordinates, device serial, and timestamp embedded in every phone photo. Store processed file only — original is discarded.

Every photo taken on a modern smartphone contains precise GPS coordinates in EXIF metadata. Storing photos without stripping EXIF means storing the precise location history of your users' social lives. This is a privacy violation and a liability. Sharp's withMetadata(false) strips all EXIF in one line.

The memory card (collage + vibe tags + prompt answer) is generated server-side. Layout defined as a React component. Satori converts JSX → SVG. Sharp converts SVG → PNG. Result stored in Supabase Storage. Shareable as an image via the standard share sheet.

Supabase Realtime hits its 2M message/month free limit around 1,000 DAU (users sending ~20 messages/day in group chats). This triggers the $25/month Supabase Pro plan upgrade. At this point you also gain the Postgres read replica and higher connection limits — it is worth the upgrade. The second trigger: Google Places exceeds the $200 monthly credit around 20k venue searches. Abstract the venue service behind venues.service.ts so switching to Foursquare (100k free calls/month) is a 1-day task.
Phone + email hybrid auth and complete first-run experience

Phone number entry is mandatory — it is the contact matching identifier that powers circle discovery. Email (via magic link or Google Sign-In) is the verification mechanism. No SMS is sent. Zero OTP cost.

Screen 1 — Phone entry: "What's your number?" Country code selector + phone input. libphonenumber-js validates format client-side. On Continue: POST /auth/start stores phone in pending_auth (15-min TTL), returns session_id.

Screen 2 — Verify: Two options shown simultaneously. "Continue with Google" (primary CTA, orange button) and email input with "Send magic link" below a divider. Google path skips the email waiting screen entirely.

Google path: Google Sign-In SDK launches. User selects account. App sends {google_id_token, session_id} to POST /auth/google. Server verifies token, retrieves phone from pending_auth, upserts users row, returns JWT pair. User is authenticated.

Email path: User enters email, taps "Send magic link". POST /auth/magic-link/send sends email via Resend. Screen 3 shows "Check your email" with the email address displayed. Resend link available after 30s. User taps link in email — deep link opens app, token verified, JWT pair returned, user authenticated.

On app open: check Expo SecureStore for refresh token. If found: call POST /auth/refresh silently (splash screen shown during this). On success: GET /users/me returns onboarding_completed, onboarding_step, last_active_tab. If onboarding complete: navigate to last_active_tab. If onboarding in progress: resume from current step. If no refresh token or token expired: show phone entry screen (phone pre-filled from MMKV — user only needs to tap Continue).
TEXT, encrypted at rest (pgcrypto AES-256). Never returned in API responses. Shown only to the account owner in settings.
TEXT UNIQUE, SHA-256(E.164_phone + SERVER_PEPPER). Indexed. Used for contact matching. SERVER_PEPPER stored in environment secrets only.
TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL. Primary auth identifier.
TEXT UNIQUE nullable. Google sub field for returning Google users.
SMALLINT DEFAULT 0. 0–6. Only ever increments. Server-side — survives reinstall.
BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE. Set true at step 6. Permanent.
TIMESTAMP nullable. Set when first prompt answered. The activation event.
id, session_id UUID, phone_number TEXT, expires_at TIMESTAMP (15 min). Rows deleted on successful auth or expiry.
id, email, token_hash (SHA-256 of raw token), expires_at (15 min), used_at nullable. Single-use.

id, user_id FK, token_hash (SHA-256), family_id UUID, expires_at (90 days), used_at nullable, device_hint TEXT. Token reuse detection: if used_at is set and token is presented again, invalidate all tokens with the same family_id.

Under 3 minutes from download to the first prompt answered. Every step either collects data the app needs to function OR shows the user something valuable. No feature tours. No tutorial carousels. The first real use of the app is the best demonstration of it.
Phone entry + verification. Sets onboarding_step = 0. Creates users row.
Display name pre-filled from Google if available (editable). Photo from camera roll optional. Fraunces heading: "What do people call you?" Sets onboarding_step = 1.
Full-screen scrollable tag grid by category. Must select ≥3 before Next appears. These power Discovery matching — non-negotiable. Sets onboarding_step = 2.
3 text fields. First prompted ("Your most niche interest?"). Skip link visible. Sets onboarding_step = 3.

Custom framing screen first: "Who else is already here? We never store your contacts." On grant: contacts hashed client-side (SHA-256 via libphonenumber-js normalisation + crypto.subtle), hashes sent to POST /users/me/contact-sync, matches returned. Matches screen shows found users with "Add to circle" (pre-selected, one tap to deselect). If OS permission denied: invite link screen shown instead. Sets contact_sync_granted, onboarding_step = 4.

Shareable invite card with user's name + avatar + invite link. One-tap share sheet. Optional phone number field: "Add your number so friends can find you automatically." Phone hashed server-side immediately. Skip link visible. Sets onboarding_step = 5.

Not a tutorial. The real Discovery screen loads with today's prompt card. A single one-time warm tooltip: "Answer this and see who in your circle thinks the same." Dismisses on any tap, never shown again. Sets onboarding_completed = true. First prompt answered fires activated_at.
No feature tours. No carousel walkthroughs. No permission pre-screens beyond what is needed.

Three contextual tooltips only, each shown once: (1) Prompt card on Discovery — "Answer and see who agrees." (2) FAB on Motives on first visit with no Motives — points at FAB. (3) Dice icon in first chat thread — "Roll for a conversation starter."

The first session must produce one meaningful social moment. If the user has circle members from contact sync, they will see matches on the first prompt reveal. If they are the first-responder (no matches yet), they see the warm "You're first" card and the notification opt-in toggle — pre-toggled ON.
Onboarding state stored server-side (users.onboarding_step). App resumes from current step after reinstall or device change.
All seven V1 mechanisms and the complete people graph

Seven mechanisms covering every realistic way someone would want to connect with a person they already know or have social proximity to. No mechanism enables meeting total strangers. All surface people with existing social context.

Contacts hashed client-side with SHA-256 + libphonenumber-js normalisation. Hashes sent to server. Server compares against phone_hash column in users table. Matches returned as user profiles. Raw contact data never leaves the device, never touches the server. Best for: people you already text.

Postgres pg_trgm trigram search on display_name and username columns. Handles partial matches and case-insensitivity. Results filtered by social distance: exact @username match always shown; display name match only shown within 2 degrees of the searcher's graph. Prevents strangers finding users by name alone. Best for: classmates, coworkers, club members you've met but don't have the number of.

Unique URL: icebreaker.app/join/[code]. Code maps to inviter's user_id in invite_links table. New user signs up via link → auto-confirmed circle connection (no pending state). Existing user taps link → circle request. Invite card generated server-side as a shareable image (user avatar + name + 3 vibe tags). Best for: bringing existing friend groups onto the platform.

Personal QR encodes the same invite link URL. Full-screen QR shown from Profile tab → QR icon. In-app camera scanner (Expo Camera) in search bar for existing users — instant circle request without leaving the app. Native camera fallback opens browser then redirects. Best for: in-person events, freshers week, meetups, campus move-in day.

Pre-computed every 6 hours via Inngest job. Candidates scored on: mutual friend count (35%), shared vibe tags (30%), mutual Motive attendance (20%), prompt answer similarity (10%), mutual connection recency (5%). Results stored in fof_suggestions table. Discovery tab shows top FOF cards with mutual friend named as context. Best for: expanding circles naturally over time.

Admin creates a named group circle. 6-character alphanumeric code generated. Shareable as text, link, or QR (designed for projection/printing). Joining the circle connects the member to all other members in the Discovery layer. Requires_approval BOOL — admin can review joins. Best for: clubs, societies, sports teams, flatmates, course cohorts, internship groups.

Already in the Discovery screen spec. Seeing which adjacent-network users answered similarly creates a natural connection moment. "Start a Motive" CTA on prompt reveal also pre-selects matched users as invitees — the circle request is sent simultaneously with the Motive invite. Best for: converting Discovery engagement into real connections.

"I'm here" mode — opt-in, time-limited (2 hours), venue-anchored. User selects their current venue from a Google Places search. They appear to other Icebreaker users also in "I'm here" mode at the same venue. No GPS coordinates stored — only the venue place_id. No background location access. Mode auto-expires. Only mutual opt-in users are visible to each other. Not built in V1 — QR code covers the in-person use case adequately.
FOF suggestion cards in a horizontal scroll strip below the prompt stack. Labelled "People you might know." Prompt match reveals include "Add to circle" contextual CTA.

Dedicated section for all connection management: search bar, contact sync status + re-sync, FOF feed, group circles, QR generator, invite link generator. Not a fifth tab — accessed from Profile to keep the bottom bar at 4 tabs.
"Others who might be down" horizontal strip powered by FOF + vibe matching. Adding someone here sends a circle request simultaneously with the Motive invite.
"Add to your circle?" for Motive attendees not yet in personal circle. Warmest possible connection moment — you just shared an experience.
Contact sync match reveal. The first and most important friend discovery moment. Determines baseline circle size and early retention.
TEXT UNIQUE nullable. 3–20 chars, alphanumeric + underscore. pg_trgm indexed. Set during onboarding or from Profile settings.
user_id FK, suggested_user_id FK, score DECIMAL(5,2), mutual_friend_ids UUID[], shared_tag_count INT, computed_at TIMESTAMP. Pre-computed. Recomputed every 6h for users with ≥3 circle members.
id, user_id FK, code TEXT UNIQUE (6-char), click_count INT DEFAULT 0, signup_count INT DEFAULT 0, accepted_count INT DEFAULT 0, created_at. Tracks per-link virality for ambassador identification.
id, name TEXT, admin_user_id FK, join_code TEXT UNIQUE (6-char), join_link_id FK, requires_approval BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, max_members INT nullable, created_at.
group_circle_id FK, user_id FK, status ENUM(pending, active), joined_at, invited_by FK nullable. PK: (group_circle_id, user_id).
First-responder handling and duplicate Motive resolution
The first user to answer a daily prompt has no matches yet. The empty state must reward early answering (they took a social risk) and bring them back when the moment becomes meaningful.
Card flips to reveal matched friends. CTA appears. Normal happy path.
State B — First in personal circle

No circle matches. Show 1–2 FOF adjacents who answered similarly at reduced prominence: "Nobody in your circle yet — but these people nearby think the same." Show the "You're first" card below. Notification toggle pre-set to ON.
State C — First in entire network
No matches anywhere. Illustrated warm card: "Bold take. You might be rarer than you think." Notification toggle pre-set to ON. Swipe up to next prompt easily accessible.
Push notification: "[Name] agrees with your [topic] take — tap to see what they said." Deep-links to the revealed prompt card.
Push notification: "3 people in your circle agree — time for a Motive?" Deep-links to prompt card with Motive CTA visible.
2 per prompt per user. No notification on 2nd match or 4th+ match.

Prompt notifications expire at midnight local time. A match arriving after midnight is never notified — the social context is gone. Quiet hours (10pm–8am): hold notification until 8am, but if 8am is the morning after the prompt day, cancel entirely.
Users can disable prompt match notifications in Settings. The toggle on the "You're first" card is a shortcut to this setting.

prompt_response_notifications: user_id FK, prompt_id FK, notifications_sent SMALLINT DEFAULT 0, first_match_notified_at TIMESTAMP nullable, threshold_notified_at TIMESTAMP nullable, opted_in BOOL DEFAULT TRUE, expires_at TIMESTAMP (midnight local).

When two users in the same network independently create Motives from the same prompt, same category, within 4 hours of each other, and with 2+ overlapping invitees — a collision is detected and the overlapping invitees' RSVP cards are held until resolved.

Runs every 60 seconds via Inngest. Finds Motive pairs where: same origin_prompt_id, same category, scheduled_at within 4 hours of each other, ≥2 overlapping users in motive_attendees. Creates motive_collisions row. Sets held_by_collision_id on overlapping attendees' motive_attendees rows.

Maximum 2 hours. Prevents duplicate RSVPs reaching overlapping invitees. If unresolved after 2 hours: hold released, both Motives proceed independently, overlapping invitees see both RSVP cards with "Two plans — join whichever feels right" framing.
In-app banner only (not push) on the Motive detail screen: "[Name] is also planning a [category] Motive tonight with some of your people. Want to join forces?" Warm, non-competitive framing.

(A) "Invite [Name] to mine — combine the groups." (B) "Join [Name]'s instead." (C) "Keep mine separate." All shown equally — no option labelled as correct. Option B only shown if other Motive has more confirmed attendees.
App asks: "Whose Motive should be the main one?" Shows both with attendee count and creation time. Winner's Motive survives. Other archived. Both creators become co-organisers. All invitees merged.
Hold released. Overlapping invitees receive both cards with the "two plans" framing. Joining one auto-declines the other silently — neither organiser sees the pass count.
If invite lists are nearly identical, skip the three-option screen. Show direct: "You and [Name] are planning the same thing with the same people. Join forces?" Yes / Keep mine.

motive_collisions: id, motive_a_id FK, motive_b_id FK, prompt_id FK, overlapping_user_ids UUID[], status ENUM(pending/resolved_merged/resolved_separate/resolved_one_joined/expired), creator_a_choice ENUM nullable, creator_b_choice ENUM nullable, surviving_motive_id FK nullable, hold_expires_at TIMESTAMP, detected_at. motive_attendees: + held_by_collision_id UUID FK nullable.

id UUID PK, email TEXT UNIQUE, email_verified BOOL, auth_provider ENUM(google/email/apple), google_id TEXT UNIQUE nullable, phone_number TEXT nullable (encrypted), phone_hash TEXT UNIQUE nullable (SHA-256+pepper), phone_verified BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, display_name TEXT, avatar_url TEXT, username TEXT UNIQUE nullable, bio TEXT, availability_status ENUM(down_to_hang/busy/ask_me), onboarding_step SMALLINT DEFAULT 0, onboarding_completed BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, onboarding_completed_at TIMESTAMP nullable, contact_sync_granted BOOL nullable, activated_at TIMESTAMP nullable, first_motive_at TIMESTAMP nullable, last_active_tab ENUM(discovery/motives/chat/profile), created_at, updated_at
id UUID PK, session_id UUID UNIQUE, phone_number TEXT, expires_at TIMESTAMP (15 min). Deleted on auth success or expiry.
id UUID PK, email TEXT, token_hash TEXT, expires_at TIMESTAMP, used_at TIMESTAMP nullable, created_at
id UUID PK, user_id FK, token_hash TEXT, family_id UUID, expires_at TIMESTAMP (90 days), used_at TIMESTAMP nullable, device_hint TEXT, created_at
id UUID PK, user_id FK, friend_id FK, status ENUM(pending/confirmed), created_at. Bidirectional — one row per direction. Indexed: (user_id, status), (friend_id, status).
id, name TEXT, admin_user_id FK, join_code TEXT UNIQUE (6-char), requires_approval BOOL, max_members INT nullable, created_at
group_circle_id FK, user_id FK, status ENUM(pending/active), joined_at. PK: (group_circle_id, user_id).
user_id FK, suggested_user_id FK, score DECIMAL, mutual_friend_ids UUID[], shared_tag_count INT, computed_at. PK: (user_id, suggested_user_id). Recomputed every 6h.
id, user_id FK, code TEXT UNIQUE, click_count INT, signup_count INT, accepted_count INT, created_at
user_id FK, target_user_id FK, score DECIMAL, updated_at. Higher score = less frequent appearance in Discovery.
id, blocker_id FK, blocked_id FK, created_at. Server-enforced across all surfaces.
id, muter_id FK, muted_id FK, created_at. Suppresses Motive invite push notifications from muted user.

id, creator_id FK, title TEXT, category ENUM, description TEXT, scheduled_at TIMESTAMP, venue_place_id TEXT, venue_name TEXT, status ENUM(open/locked/completed/cancelled/unconfirmed), is_journey BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, journey_type ENUM(free_roam/day_out/itinerary) nullable, journey_started_at nullable, journey_ended_at nullable, origin_prompt_id FK nullable, collision_status ENUM(none/pending/resolved) DEFAULT none, created_at
motive_id FK, user_id FK, role ENUM(organiser/co_organiser/attendee), rsvp_status ENUM(invited/joined/passed), responded_at, held_by_collision_id FK nullable. PK: (motive_id, user_id).

id, motive_id FK, position INT, category ENUM, venue_place_id nullable, venue_name nullable, scheduled_at nullable, time_of_day ENUM(morning/afternoon/evening) nullable, notes TEXT nullable, status ENUM(planned/current/completed/skipped), checked_in_at nullable, completed_at nullable, is_partner_venue BOOL DEFAULT FALSE
stop_id FK, user_id FK, checked_in_at TIMESTAMP. Manual only — no GPS. PK: (stop_id, user_id).

id, motive_a_id FK, motive_b_id FK, prompt_id FK nullable, overlapping_user_ids UUID[], status ENUM, creator_a_choice ENUM nullable, creator_b_choice ENUM nullable, surviving_motive_id FK nullable, hold_expires_at TIMESTAMP, detected_at

id, business_name, place_id TEXT, category ENUM, is_active BOOL, min_rating DECIMAL (≥4.0 enforced), base_deal_description TEXT nullable, group_deal_description TEXT nullable, min_group_size INT nullable, priority_score INT, created_at
id, motive_id FK, stop_id FK nullable, user_id FK, venue_place_id TEXT, rating SMALLINT (1/2/3), consented_to_google_cross_post BOOL, google_review_posted BOOL, created_at
id, label TEXT, emoji TEXT, category ENUM, created_at
user_id FK, tag_id FK. PK: (user_id, tag_id).
id, question TEXT, category ENUM, active_date DATE UNIQUE, created_at
user_id FK, prompt_id FK, response_text TEXT, responded_at. PK: (user_id, prompt_id). Indexed: (prompt_id, responded_at).

user_id FK, prompt_id FK, notifications_sent SMALLINT DEFAULT 0, first_match_notified_at nullable, threshold_notified_at nullable, opted_in BOOL DEFAULT TRUE, expires_at TIMESTAMP. PK: (user_id, prompt_id).
motive_id FK, stop_id FK nullable (for journey stop tagging), tag ENUM, count INT DEFAULT 0. PK: (motive_id, tag).
id, type ENUM(motive_thread/group), motive_id FK nullable, name TEXT nullable, created_at
chat_id FK, user_id FK, joined_at, last_read_at. PK: (chat_id, user_id).
id, chat_id FK, sender_id FK, content TEXT, type ENUM(text/prompt_bubble/poll/system), metadata JSONB nullable, created_at. Indexed: (chat_id, created_at DESC).
id, motive_id FK, generated_at, cover_photo_url TEXT, vibe_tags TEXT[], prompt_question TEXT, prompt_answer TEXT, attendee_ids UUID[], format ENUM(single/chapter/timeline), created_at
id, motive_id FK, stop_id FK nullable, uploaded_by FK, photo_url TEXT, created_at. Upload window: 24h from motive.journey_ended_at or motive.scheduled_at.
id, motive_id FK, user_id FK, scheduled_at TIMESTAMP (motive_date + 14 days), sent_at nullable, status ENUM(pending/sent/cancelled)
id, reporter_id FK, content_type ENUM(message/profile/photo/motive), content_id UUID, reason TEXT, status ENUM(pending/reviewed/actioned), created_at. Internal only — never in third-party analytics.
id, user_id FK, action ENUM(warning/suspension/ban), reason TEXT, actioned_by TEXT, created_at. Append-only.

place_id TEXT, period_start DATE, impression_count INT, selection_count INT, avg_rating DECIMAL, vibe_tag_distribution JSONB, avg_group_size DECIMAL, group_deal_conversions INT. Aggregated weekly — no individual user data.
What to build first and why
Monorepo setup: Turborepo, apps/mobile (Expo), packages/api (Hono), packages/shared (Drizzle schema + shared types)
Supabase project setup: run all schema migrations, enable pg_trgm extension, configure Row Level Security policies
Design system: Fraunces + DM Sans fonts, color tokens, Reanimated 3 base config, Bottom Sheet library
Base components: Button, Card, Pill tag, Avatar, Input, Bottom sheet wrapper
React Navigation v6: 4-tab bottom navigator with placeholder screens + native-stack per tab
Self-managed auth: all 6 endpoints + validateJWT middleware + refresh token rotation
Google Sign-In SDK integration (Expo Google Sign-In)
Resend magic link email: React Email template + send endpoint
Fly.io deployment: Dockerfile, fly.toml, CI/CD via GitHub Actions
Onboarding flow: all 6 steps, server-side step persistence, contact sync (Expo Contacts + client-side hashing)
Username search: pg_trgm index, social-distance filtered search endpoint
QR code: generation (react-native-qrcode-svg), in-app scanner (Expo Camera), invite link flow
Vibe tags: seed data, tag selection UI, user_vibe_tags writes
Circles system: request, accept, list, block, mute
Daily prompts: CMS seed data (60 prompts), distribution endpoint, response submission
Prompt match reveal: match query, card flip animation (Reanimated 3), first-responder empty state
Match notifications: prompt_response_notifications table, Inngest job, threshold logic, expiry at midnight
Discovery tab: prompt card stack, FOF strip (using contact-sync matches initially before FOF job), circle pulse
Motive creation flow: FAB, bottom sheet, category grid, people selection (all 7 discovery mechanisms), details screen
RSVP card: swipe gesture (Gesture Handler), particle burst animation, push notification trigger
Supabase Realtime: subscribe to motive channel for live RSVP updates, live attendee count
Venue picker: Google Places API integration, promoted venue injection, voting UI
Motive collision detection: Inngest 60-second job, hold logic, in-app notification, three-option resolution UI
Journey Motives: is_journey flag, stop creation UI, drag-to-reorder (Drag-and-drop library), live view with Realtime check-ins
Chat: Supabase Realtime subscription per chat_id, message bubbles, offline recovery (since= query), message ordering
Supabase Presence: typing indicators, online status per chat
Dice icebreaker injector: random prompt endpoint, prompt bubble card in chat
General group chat creation: manual + "Start a group?" suggestion after repeat Motive attendees
Post-Motive flow: all 6 steps (confirmation → vibe tags → photo upload → venue rating → Google cross-post → memory card)
Sharp image pipeline: resize, WebP conversion, EXIF stripping on upload
Satori memory card generation: JSX layout → SVG → PNG → Supabase Storage
Memory resurfacing: Inngest scheduled job at T+14d, push notification, deep link to pre-populated Motive create
FOF suggestion algorithm: Inngest 6-hour recompute job, 5-signal scoring, fof_suggestions table
Group circles: create, join via code, admin approval flow, circle QR code
Full animation pass: prompt card flip, RSVP swipe, FAB spring, tag selection, memory card save
Social cooling: automatic FOF dampening after repeated passes/mutes
Content moderation: report flow, Inngest moderation queue job, admin dashboard (read-only Retool or simple Next.js)
PostHog event instrumentation: all events from analytics spec
Sentry PII scrubbing configuration, performance tracing on slow API endpoints
Accessibility pass: accessible labels, 44×44 tap targets, Dynamic Type (iOS) / font scaling (Android)
EAS Build configuration for App Store and Play Store submission
The single most important thing to build first in Phase 1

The prompt match reveal with the first-responder "You're first" state. This is the moment that either hooks a new user or loses them forever. Get this right before building anything else. It should feel warm, not empty — the illustrated card, the notification toggle pre-set to ON, the "you might be rarer than you think" copy. Test it with real users from day one.
